sdk_docs_evidence: Langfuse Python SDK v4 baseline from Context7 (2026-05-07): @observe supports sync/async; capture_input/output=False valid; propagate_attributes propagates user/session/tags/metadata; explicit trace IDs via langfuse_trace_id header

SDK registry (docs/engineering/sdk-registry.md): telegram_bot/observability.py is central module; @observe(name=..., capture_input=False, capture_output=False) on nodes/steps; propagate_attributes() wraps entry-points; langfuse.openai.AsyncOpenAI is adopted drop-in

new_bugs.title: Stale has-pr label on #1253 after PR #1290 closed unmerged
new_bugs.impact: Issue tracking accuracy: #1253 still appears as having an open PR, confusing sprint planning and worker dispatch
new_bugs.evidence: gh pr view 1290 --json state,mergedAt returns {"state":"CLOSED","mergedAt":null}; issue #1253 labels include 'has-pr'
new_bugs.reproduction: gh issue view 1253 --json labels; gh pr view 1290 --json state,mergedAt
new_bugs.expected: #1253 should not have 'has-pr' label when its associated PR is closed unmerged
new_bugs.actual: #1253 retains 'has-pr' label and a 2026-05-04 comment asserting PR #1290 is open

new_bugs.title: src/contextualization/openai.py uses plain openai.AsyncOpenAI instead of langfuse.openai.AsyncOpenAI
new_bugs.impact: LLM calls in contextualization path bypass Langfuse auto-tracing, creating blind spots even though @observe decorates the function boundary
new_bugs.evidence: src/contextualization/openai.py line 4: from openai import AsyncOpenAI; line 25: self.client = AsyncOpenAI(...). SDK registry says 'langfuse.openai.AsyncOpenAI is the adopted drop-in'
new_bugs.expected: Contextualization providers should use langfuse.openai.AsyncOpenAI for auto-tracing, or have an explicit ADR documenting why plain client is required
new_bugs.actual: Plain openai.AsyncOpenAI is used; inner LLM call is not auto-traced by Langfuse

new_bugs.title: Local dev stack non-functional due to stale images, Postgres password drift, invalid Langfuse secrets
new_bugs.impact: Blocks all local Langfuse trace validation work for #1307, #1253-E, and any runtime observability testing
new_bugs.evidence: docs/audits/2026-05-07-docker-langfuse-health-audit.md: dev_bot image built 2026-04-17 with Python 3.14; langfuse v4 crashes with pydantic.v1 ConfigError. Postgres data volume password mismatch causes Prisma P1000. ENCRYPTION_KEY is 19 chars (needs 64 hex).
new_bugs.reproduction: docker run --rm dev_bot python -c 'import langfuse' # ConfigError. docker logs dev_langfuse_1 # P1000. docker logs dev_langfuse-worker_1 # ZodError ENCRYPTION_KEY too small.
new_bugs.expected: Local make local-up and make run-bot should produce healthy containers and OTEL 200 responses
new_bugs.actual: bot, langfuse, langfuse-worker all in restart loops; Langfuse UI unreachable
